violations.text: Allergen statement is lacking on the specials menu. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Date must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025
Specials menu is lacking a consumer advisory. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration units. All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer inside the unit that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwashers and the quaternary ammonium concentration for the 3-bay sink/sanitizer buckets. Test strips must be obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025

Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. Date and time of the last sale were lacking. Establishment must mark all shellstock tags with the date and time of the last sale. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/18/2024
The ceiling in the storage area was observed in poor repair. Physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Shellstock clams located in the raw bar lowboy did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. Tag was located at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2024

Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. Date and time of the last sale were lacking. Establishment must mark all shellstock tags with the date and time of the last sale. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils can opener, both ice machines, potato slicer, and deli slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Both ice machines were emptied at the time of inspection. Establishment must clean and sanitize the following food contact surfaces before using. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was observed over 400 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-300 ppm. Establishment is to have the sanitizer dispenser serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2024

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Spray bottles were labeled at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2024
The gaskets for the prep unit pizza lowboy were observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/26/2024

-The prep sink in the basement was observed leaking into a container. -The walk- in cooler fan was observed leaking into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024
The ceiling in the storage area was observed in poor repair. Physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/26/2024
The sinks in the basement were observed leaking onto the floor and into a bucket. Through interview with PIC. Service is to be done on the sink tomorrow. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2023
Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. Time and date of last used were lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (potato slicer, and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Establishment turned off the ice machine and is using bagged ice until the machine is cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

Prep unit 3 was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Misc. Food items was observed held at 47 D F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Establishment is not to use prep unit until it has been repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

Reduced oxygen packed lobster meat was observed thawing in the walk in cooler. The internal temperature of the lobster meat exceeded 38 degrees Fahrenheit. All fish products in reduced oxygen packaging must have air re-introduced before thawing. Establishment voluntarily disposed of lobster meat. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by small flys in the basement and throughout the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to work with pest control. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
Food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler and walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

Food items were observed uncovered in the walk in cooler and walk in freezer. Unless during the cooling process, food items should be covered while being stored to prevent contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

Refrigerator door gaskets on all of the kitchen prep units were observed in poor repair. The handle on prep unit two is missing. Food equipment must be maintained in good repari. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2023

The prep sink in the basement was observed leaking onto the floor and into a bucket. All liquids must drain into a drain. The plumbing system must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
